title: Nights
description: Portraits and landscapes shot in the last hour of daylight.
aiPrompt: Generate a Bret Easton Ellis style short text inspired by the picture it is attached to. Max characters 280.

photos.caption: They stood in the tagged hallway holding dark drinks in plastic cups, nobody really listening to anyone, everyone performing something. The graffiti was more honest than the conversation. Someone mentioned Berlin. Someone always mentions Berlin.

We stumbled out of the party into the cold, fur coats and cigarette smoke, laughing at nothing. The city didn't care. It never does. She said something about leaving Paris tomorrow but tomorrow never really comes for people like us.

The bar smelled like ambition and regret. I ordered another beer I wouldn't finish. Bon Jovi watched from the wall with that permanent optimism I'd lost somewhere between my second marriage and my third Klonopin. Everyone here was performing. I was just better dressed.

the flash catches us mid-collapse, someone's visor reflecting nothing worth seeing. we're all moving too fast to be remembered. tyler says something about leaving but nobody leaves. the room smells like red bull and someone else's perfume and the end of something.

She moved through the party like something already gone. The lights bled warm and chemical. I watched her disappear into the hallway and felt nothing, which was the problem. Someone said my name. I didn't turn around. The music kept going. Everything keeps going.

The grillz caught light like something expensive dying. Marcus held his drink the way you hold evidence. I couldn't remember whose party this was or whose blood was on the bathroom floor. Nobody cared. The DJ played something nihilistic and we leaned into it like a wound.

The lights behind him blur like memories he'd rather forget. He adjusts his collar—Etro, probably—and stares past everyone. Someone is talking about real estate. Someone is always talking about real estate. He thinks about the pill dissolving under his tongue and waits for nothing.

title: Nocturnal Animals
description: Long-exposure urban photography shot between midnight and 4am.
aiPrompt: Generate a Bret Easton Ellis style short text inspired by the picture it is attached to. Max characters 280.

photos.caption: Three kids outside the party, teeth bright in the flash, eyes not quite focused. Someone said something about tomorrow but nobody really listened. The night was expensive and free at the same time. We were all pretending we weren't cold.

She stared into the lens like it owed her something. Glitter on her cheeks like the residue of a party that ended three days ago. The red balaclava smelled like cigarettes and someone else's cologne. She hadn't slept. None of us had. It didn't matter anymore.

She stood there under the tiki lights wearing a straw hat that cost nothing and earrings that meant even less, and I thought about how everyone at this party was performing something they'd never rehearsed. The DJ played another song nobody asked for. Nobody left.

He stood in the doorway of the club on Avenue B, pupils catching flash like an animal's, chain glinting, mouth half-open as if the next sentence would ruin someone's life. Nobody asked his name. Nobody needed to. The night was already dissolving into something transactional.

She's wearing leopard print and a medusa piercing and her skin is raw under the flash and she doesn't blink. Someone behind me says she's somebody. I finish my drink. The DJ plays something nobody asked for. I think about leaving. I don't leave.

We stood outside the party pretending we didn't care about anything. Her Bayern jersey was ironic. My indifference wasn't. The flash made us look like suspects. Somewhere a phone buzzed and neither of us reached for it. The night smelled like jasmine and something ending.

She stared through me at the VIP section, pupils like black markets, piercings catching strobe light like tiny signals from a planet where feeling something still meant something. I ordered another vodka. She looked expensive in ways that had nothing to do with money.

She stares into the flash like it owes her something. Butterfly on her collarbone, glitter on her throat. Somebody's drink is getting warm on a table she already left. The night is expensive and means nothing. She knows this. She came anyway.
